symptom: a knee X-ray showing the lower thigh bone flaring out widely at the end and shaped like a laboratory flask
hook: the thigh bone forgot to slim down and grew a flask shape instead
whatYouSee: The ends of the long bones look widened and under-shaped like the base of a flask.
whyItMatters: A rare bone modeling disorder stops the metaphysis from narrowing as the child grows. Failed remodeling can point to storage diseases like Gaucher or to bone dysplasias.
treatment: There is no fix for the shape itself and care targets the underlying cause. Gaucher disease is treated with enzyme replacement and dysplasias are watched over time.
takeaway: Bone has to be sculpted as it grows and sometimes the sculpting fails.

condition.symptom: a knee X-ray showing the lower thigh bone flaring out widely at the end and shaped like a laboratory flask
condition.hook: the thigh bone forgot to slim down and grew a flask shape instead
condition.keyFindings: AP view of both knees where the lower end of each thigh bone widens and flares outward and fails to narrow into the normal shaft so the metaphysis looks like the rounded base of a laboratory flask with a thin outer cortex over the flared area and symmetric involvement of both knees
condition.whatYouSee: The ends of the long bones look widened and under-shaped like the base of a flask.
condition.whyItMatters: A rare bone modeling disorder stops the metaphysis from narrowing as the child grows. Failed remodeling can point to storage diseases like Gaucher or to bone dysplasias.
condition.treatment: There is no fix for the shape itself and care targets the underlying cause. Gaucher disease is treated with enzyme replacement and dysplasias are watched over time.
condition.takeaway: Bone has to be sculpted as it grows and sometimes the sculpting fails.

laypersonQuestion: Does that bone end look wider than it should?
seedHint: Look at where the thigh bone meets the knee and ask yourself if it flares or stays straight.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a knee X-ray showing the lower thigh bone flaring out widely at the end and shaped like a laboratory flask.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And the thigh bone forgot to slim down and grew a flask shape instead.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Erlenmeyer flask deformity

👀 What you see:
The ends of the long bones look widened and under-shaped like the base of a flask.

💊 Treatment:
There is no fix for the shape itself and care targets the underlying cause. Gaucher disease is treated with enzyme replacement and dysplasias are watched over time.
